question_text: How would you design an electromagnet that could pick up steel objects from a distance? Explain your answer using two factors that affect electromagnet strength.
correct_answer: You would increase the current (using a higher voltage battery) and increase the number of coils in the wire. A stronger magnetic field reaches further distances and can attract steel more powerfully.
explanation: Electromagnet strength depends on two main factors: the amount of current flowing through the wire (more current = stronger field) and the number of coils (more coils = stronger field). Both factors multiply their effects, so increasing either one makes the electromagnet stronger and able to pick up steel from greater distances.
